records.ety: [Prob. akin to mumble: cf. also F. manger to eat (cf. Mange), and mcher to cher (cf. Masticate). See Mumble.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Munched (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Munching.]

defs: To chew with a grinding, crunching sound, as a beast chews provender; to chew deliberately or in large mouthfuls.
quotes: I could munch your good dry oats.
